title: USAGE Agent Manifest Schema
description: JSON Schema (Draft 7) for USAGE-compliant agent deployment manifests. Defines the structure, constraints, and validation rules for AgentManifest.yaml files that describe autonomous agent workloads.

apiVersion.description: API version of the manifest specification. Currently must be 'usage.io/v1' for USAGE v1.0 compliance.

kind.description: Kind identifies the type of resource being defined. Must be 'SovereignAgent' for agent workload manifests.

metadata.description: Metadata provides identifying and organizational information about the agent.

name.description: Name is the unique identifier for this agent within its namespace. Must be a valid DNS subdomain name (lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only).

labels.description: Labels are key-value pairs for organizing and selecting agents. Common label categories: environment (dev, staging, prod), team (data-science, platform), and domain (nlp, vision, agentic).

spec.description: Spec defines the runtime configuration, security model, and resource constraints for the agent.

runtime.description: Runtime specifies the agent's execution profile and resource quotas.

profile.description: Profile is a named runtime configuration template. Predefined profiles: 'lightweight' (single-turn, <1M tokens), 'standard' (multi-turn, <10M tokens), 'heavyweight' (long-running, <100M tokens), 'custom' (user-defined constraints).

maxTokensQuota.description: MaxTokensQuota is the maximum number of tokens the agent is allowed to consume per session. Enforced by the runtime; exceeding this quota triggers termination with SIG_AGENT_TERMINATE.

maxConcurrentSessions.description: MaxConcurrentSessions limits the number of simultaneous agent instances. Defaults to 1. Set to 0 for unlimited.

sessionTimeoutSeconds.description: SessionTimeoutSeconds is the maximum wall-clock time an agent session can run. After this duration, the substrate sends SIG_AGENT_TERMINATE. Set to 0 for no timeout (not recommended for production).

maxToolNestingDepth.description: MaxToolNestingDepth limits the maximum nesting depth of tool invocations. Prevents stack exhaustion and infinite recursion. Defaults to 10.

security.description: Security specifies the agent's security isolation level and authorized tool capabilities.

sandboxLevel.description: SandboxLevel defines the strength of security isolation. 'strict' (maximum isolation, minimal substrate access), 'moderate' (balanced isolation), 'permissive' (minimal isolation, broad substrate access). Defaults to 'moderate'.

allowedTools.description: AllowedTools is a list of tool registry names the agent is permitted to invoke. Tool names follow the pattern 'namespace.tool_name'. The substrate denies invocations of unlisted tools at the UsageCallTool boundary.

requiresApprovalFor.description: RequiresApprovalFor is an optional list of tool names that require explicit human approval before execution. Useful for high-risk operations (system writes, financial transactions).

networkAccess.description: NetworkAccess specifies the agent's outbound network permissions. 'none' (no external network), 'restricted' (approved domains only), 'open' (unrestricted). Defaults to 'restricted'.

fileSystemAccess.description: FileSystemAccess specifies the agent's file system permissions. 'none' (no access), 'read-only' (read-only access to workspace), 'read-write' (read-write access to workspace). Defaults to 'read-only'.

memory.description: Memory specifies the agent's memory configuration across the three-tier storage model.

l1ContextWindow.description: L1ContextWindow is the size of the hot, in-process context window in tokens. Limited by the model's maximum context length. Common values: 8000 (GPT-3.5), 100000 (GPT-4 Turbo), 200000 (advanced models).

l2CacheProvider.description: L2CacheProvider is a URI pointing to the warm cache backend. Format: 'provider://host:port/path'. Examples: 'redis://cache-server:6379', 'postgres://pgvector-db:5432/embeddings', 'local://tmp/cache'.

l3ColdStorageProvider.description: L3ColdStorageProvider is a URI pointing to the cold storage backend. Format: 'provider://bucket/path'. Examples: 's3://my-bucket/agents/session-data', 'gcs://my-bucket/cold-storage', 'file:///persistent/storage'.

l2CachePolicy.description: L2CachePolicy defines the eviction strategy for warm cache. 'lru' (least-recently-used), 'lfu' (least-frequently-used), 'ttl' (time-to-live). Defaults to 'lru'.

l2CacheTtlSeconds.description: L2CacheTtlSeconds defines the time-to-live for items in the warm cache (if L2CachePolicy is 'ttl'). Set to 0 for indefinite TTL.

identity.description: Identity specifies the agent's identity context and authentication configuration.

modelId.description: ModelId is the identifier of the LLM backing the agent. Examples: 'claude-3.5-sonnet', 'gpt-4-turbo', 'open-llama-70b'.

systemPrompt.description: SystemPrompt is the system-level instruction provided to the model. This defines the agent's core personality and behavioral constraints.
examples: You are a helpful research assistant...

authMethod.description: AuthMethod specifies how the agent authenticates with the substrate. 'api-key' (API key), 'oauth' (OAuth 2.0), 'service-account' (service account), 'mTLS' (mutual TLS).

monitoring.description: Monitoring specifies observability and logging configuration for the agent.

loggingLevel.description: LoggingLevel controls verbosity of runtime logs. 'debug' (detailed), 'info' (informational), 'warn' (warnings and errors), 'error' (errors only).

metricsEnabled.description: MetricsEnabled controls whether runtime metrics (latency, token consumption, etc.) are collected and exported.

tracingEnabled.description: TracingEnabled controls whether distributed tracing (e.g., via OpenTelemetry) is enabled for this agent.

extensions.description: Extensions is an optional block for vendor-specific infrastructure configurations and optimizations. This block allows substrate vendors (e.g., Myelin-AX, proprietary enterprise substrates) to declare implementation-specific settings while keeping all core USAGE properties strictly standardized and portable.
